name=search_term_string"}],"inLanguage":"en-US"},{"@type":"Organization","@id":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/#organization","name":"TechCrunch","alternateName":"TC","url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","inLanguage":"en-US","@id":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/#\/schema\/logo\/image\/","url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2018\/04\/tc-logo-2018-square-reverse2x.png?resize=1200,1200","contentUrl":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2018\/04\/tc-logo-2018-square-reverse2x.png?resize=1200,1200","width":1200,"height":1200,"caption":"TechCrunch"},"image":{"@id":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/#\/schema\/logo\/image\/"},"sameAs":["http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/www.facebook.com\/techcrunch","http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/twitter.com\/TechCrunch","http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/mstdn.social\/@TechCrunch","http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/bsky.app\/profile\/techcrunch.bsky.social","http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/www.threads.net\/@techcrunch"]}]}},"_links":{"self":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tags\/429989"}],"collection":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tags"}],"about":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/taxonomies\/post_tag"}],"wp:post_type":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/posts?tags=429989"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=429989"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=429989"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=429989"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=429989"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2635228,"date":"2023-11-29T06:33:30","date_gmt":"2023-11-29T14:33:30","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2635228"},"modified":"2023-11-30T11:20:42","modified_gmt":"2023-11-30T19:20:42","slug":"gm-to-slash-spending-at-cruise-by-hundreds-of-millions-of-dollars","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/29\/gm-to-slash-spending-at-cruise-by-hundreds-of-millions-of-dollars\/","title":{"rendered":"GM to slash spending at Cruise by &#8216;hundreds of millions of dollars&#8217;"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">GM Chair and CEO Mary Barra reiterated Wednesday plans for Cruise to be more &#8220;deliberate&#8221; when operations eventually resume at the troubled self-driving vehicle subsidiary. For GM, that will include slashing spending at Cruise &#8220;by hundreds of millions of dollars&#8221; in 2024, an action that is expected to result in widespread layoffs at the San Francisco-based company that employs about 3,800 people.<\/p>\n<p>Barra and CFO Paul Jacobson said Wednesday there would be more specific information in the coming weeks about what this new Cruise will look like following the outcome of two <span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">independent<\/span> <span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">safety<\/span> <span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">and<\/span> <span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">incident<\/span> <span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">reviews that are already underway. <\/span><\/p>\n<p><span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">The pair&#8217;s comments were part of a <a href=\"https:\/\/investor.gm.com\/static-files\/e2c77dd2-6e2f-4e86-b134-a81190c9ee7d\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">broader GM update<\/a> that aimed to boost confidence in the automaker following a protracted United Autoworkers strike <\/span><span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">and softening demand for EVs.\u00a0<\/span><span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">GM said the new labor deal will cost it $9.3 billion. The company also<\/span><span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">\u00a0reinstated its 2023 guidance to include an adjusted earnings of $11.7 billion to $12.7 billion, compared to the previous outlook of $12 billion to $14 billion, an <\/span><span class=\"transcript-snippet__content__body__word ng-star-inserted\" data-highlighted=\"false\" data-playhead=\"false\">accelerated $10 billion share buyback program and a 33% increase in i<\/span>ts quarterly dividend next year to 12 cents per share.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;What Cruise accomplished over the past eight years since we acquired the company is remarkable,&#8221; Barra said during the Wednesday morning conference call. &#8220;Our priority now is to refocus them on safety, transparency and accountability and build trust with regulators at the local, state and federal levels, including first responders and the communities in which we will operate.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>Barra added that she expects &#8220;the pace of Cruise expansion to be more deliberate when operations resume and spending will be substantially lower in 2024 than it was in 2023.&#8221; Jacobson later added spending would be cut by hundreds of millions of dollars.<\/p>\n<p>GM has invested billions of dollars in Cruise since it acquired the company in March 2016. Spending has ballooned in recent years in step with Cruise&#8217;s aggressive plans to launch in more than a dozen U.S. cities. The company burned through $732 million in the third quarter of 2023, according to GM&#8217;s last earnings report.<\/p>\n<p>Barra&#8217;s comments come after weeks of disarray at Cruise following the California Department of Motor Vehicles&#8217; October 24 decision to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/24\/dmv-immediately-suspends-cruises-robotaxi-permit-in-california\/\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/24\/dmv-immediately-suspends-cruises-robotaxi-permit-in-california\/\">suspend the company&#8217;s permits<\/a> to operate self-driving vehicles on public roads after <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/03\/in-latest-cruise-incident-video-shows-pedestrian-struck-by-human-driven-car-then-run-over-by-robotaxi\/\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/03\/in-latest-cruise-incident-video-shows-pedestrian-struck-by-human-driven-car-then-run-over-by-robotaxi\/\">an incident<\/a> that saw a pedestrian \u2014 who had been initially hit by a human-driven car and landed in the path of a Cruise robotaxi \u2014 run over and dragged 20 feet by the AV. A video, which TechCrunch viewed a day after the incident, showed the robotaxi braking aggressively and coming to a stop over the woman. The DMV\u2019s order of suspension stated that Cruise withheld about seven seconds of video footage, which showed the robotaxi then attempting to pull over and subsequently dragging the woman 20 feet.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>Cruise received in August 2023 the last permit required to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/08\/10\/cruise-and-waymo-win-robotaxi-expansions-in-san-francisco\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">operate its robotaxi service<\/a> in San Francisco. Almost immediately, a number of incidents began stacking up, including videos of the robotaxis blocking traffic, driving into wet cement and a crash with an <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/08\/18\/cruise-told-by-regulators-to-immediately-reduce-robotaxi-fleet-50-following-crash\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">emergency responder vehicle<\/a>. However, it was the October 2 incident that prompted the DMV, and soon after the California Public Utilities Commission, to pull permits, effectively ending Cruise&#8217;s ability to operate in the state.<\/p>\n<p>Cruise soon paused all of its driverless operations across its fleet, including in newly launched locations in Austin, Houston and Phoenix. Last week, co-founder and CEO <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/19\/cruise-co-founder-and-ceo-kyle-vogt-resigns\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Kyle Vogt abruptly resigned<\/a>. His co-founder Dan Kan also resigned.<\/p>\n<p>GM has since circled the executive wagons by elevating some Cruise insiders and inserting its own leaders at the top.<\/p>\n<p>GM didn&#8217;t name a replacement for Vogt, instead appointing co-presidents. Mo Elshenawy, who is executive vice president of engineering at Cruise, is now CTO and co-president. Craig Glidden, a Cruise board member and GM\u2019s EVP of legal and policy who was <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/gm-inserts-exec-at-cruise-as-safety-review-expands-manual-self-driving-paused\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/gm-inserts-exec-at-cruise-as-safety-review-expands-manual-self-driving-paused\/\">recently put in charge<\/a> as chief administrative officer at Cruise, is also in the co-president role. Jon McNeill, a member of GM\u2019s board, has been appointed vice chairman of the Cruise board.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;We have a lot of confidence with what the two co-presidents will do,&#8221; Barra said. &#8220;And GM will be leaning in to make sure that it meets our strict requirements from a safety perspective.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>While specific details about Cruise&#8217;s restructuring were scant, Barra did hint that the automaker was already looking for ways to be more efficient and reduce spending prior to its recent problems. She noted that the company had found &#8220;synergies&#8221; between Cruise and what Mike Abbott, a former Apple executive leading GM&#8217;s software is working on.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>GM Chair and CEO Mary Barra reiterated Wednesday plans for Cruise to be more &#8220;deliberate&#8221; when operations eventually resume at the troubled self-driving vehicle subsidiary. For GM, that will include slashing spending at Cruise &#8220;by hundreds of millions of dollars&#8221; in 2024, an action that is expected to result in widespread layoffs at the San [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2632350,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"0d7253f5-b52e-3443-bc6e-9dfc253eb3dd","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-29T14:33:36Z","apple_news_api_id":"020da6f1-ded3-4f3d-8e2a-e5626c3adbcd","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-30T18:21:44Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAg==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AAg2m8d7TTz2OKuVibDrbzQ","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[8577009,1452,73527],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- launches leasing program for its all-electric R1T pickup in 14 states"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Rivian kicked off Monday a limited program that will give customers in select U.S. states the option to lease an all-electric R1T pickup truck.<\/p>\n<p>For now, customers living in 14 U.S. states can <a href=\"https:\/\/rivian.com\/support\/article\/does-rivian-offer-leasing\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">access the leasing program<\/a> via the R1T configurator page on its website. The states, a list that includes Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida and Texas, were chosen based on factors such as where Rivian customers are located and where leasing is most popular, according to the company. Other qualifying states include Georgia, Massachusetts, Michigan, Missouri, New Jersey, New York, Nevada, Pennsylvania and Washington.<\/p>\n<p>Rivian spokesperson Rachel Maniago said the company plans to expand its leasing program to include other models and regions over time.<\/p>\n<p>Leasing has been one of the most requested financing options, according to the company. Rivian opted to start the program with the R1T, which was the first of its EVs to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2021\/09\/14\/rivians-first-production-r1t-electric-pickup-truck-rolls-off-the-line\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">go into production<\/a>. With many of Rivian&#8217;s supply chain problems now in the rearview mirror, the company has been able to scale production of the R1T and make it more readily available.<\/p>\n<p>Leasing could give the R1T another boost. The R1S SUV has become the company&#8217;s most sought after model from customers. The leasing program may also prove popular since it is also eligible for the $7,500 federal tax credit \u2014 a <span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\">cost reduction that will be applied to their vehicle subtotal during the digital purchasing process.<\/span><\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Rivian kicked off Monday a limited program that will give customers in select U.S. states the option to lease an all-electric R1T pickup truck. For now, customers living in 14 U.S. states can access the leasing program via the R1T configurator page on its website. The states, a list that includes Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida /techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=576617966"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=576617966"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=576617966"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=576617966"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2633116,"date":"2023-11-22T15:51:26","date_gmt":"2023-11-22T23:51:26","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2633116"},"modified":"2023-11-22T15:51:48","modified_gmt":"2023-11-22T23:51:48","slug":"bruised-cruise-shifts-gears-scaled-down-robotaxi-comeback-plan","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/22\/bruised-cruise-shifts-gears-scaled-down-robotaxi-comeback-plan\/","title":{"rendered":"Bruised Cruise shifts gears in scaled-down robotaxi comeback plan"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Cruise executives are taking a measured business approach that preserves cash and improves safety culture in an attempt to put GM&#8217;s troubled autonomous vehicle subsidiary on the right path.<\/p>\n<p>The first steps in this rebuilding plan, which includes pausing production on its Origin robotaxi, were laid out in an internal email sent to employees by Mo Elshenawy, who was executive vice president of engineering at Cruise and ascended into the president role after co-founder and <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/19\/cruise-co-founder-and-ceo-kyle-vogt-resigns\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">CEO Kyle Vogt resigned<\/a>.<\/p>\n<p>For now, this strategy includes more &#8220;realistic&#8221; plans, according to Elshenawy. That means focusing on its current robotaxi platform the Chevy Bolt AV instead of the custom-built Origin shuttle that GM started producing earlier this year. GM recently temporarily paused production of Origin. This latest email notes that while development of the Origin program will continue, the vehicle will not be produced in 2024.\u00a0 The company also is reviewing its layoff plans and will provide an update in several weeks.<\/p>\n<p>While Elshenawy didn&#8217;t provide a timeline for when Cruise would restart operations, he did say the company would relaunch in just one city at first. That&#8217;s a departure from the aggressive multi-city launch strategy Cruise and GM had been focused on in 2023.<\/p>\n<p><span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\">\u201cAs we work to rebuild trust with regulators and communities, we\u2019ve made the decision to focus on the Bolt-based Cruise AVs in the near term with a longer term strategy around the Origin,&#8221; the company wrote in an emailed statement in response to the internal email. <\/span><span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\">\u201cOnce we have taken steps to improve our safety culture and rebuild trust, our strategy is to re-launch in one city and prove our performance there, before expanding.\u201d<\/span><\/p>\n<p>The internal email also provided some clarification surrounding its <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/cruise-suspends-employee-stock-program-corp-bonuses-moved-up\/\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/cruise-suspends-employee-stock-program-corp-bonuses-moved-up\/\">employee share-selling program<\/a>, which was recently suspended for the fourth quarter. Vogt had reversed this wildly unpopular decision over the weekend, but employees were still waiting for more information. The email sent Wednesday said employees who own the restricted stock units that settled from the beginning of year to October would be eligible for a new tender offer to help with tax qualifications.<\/p>\n<p>The internal email comes three days since Vogt abruptly resigned and about\u00a0a month after the California Department of Motor Vehicles <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/24\/dmv-immediately-suspends-cruises-robotaxi-permit-in-california\/\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/24\/dmv-immediately-suspends-cruises-robotaxi-permit-in-california\/\">suspended Cruise\u2019s permits<\/a>\u00a0to operate self-driving vehicles on public roads after an\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/03\/in-latest-cruise-incident-video-shows-pedestrian-struck-by-human-driven-car-then-run-over-by-robotaxi\/\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/03\/in-latest-cruise-incident-video-shows-pedestrian-struck-by-human-driven-car-then-run-over-by-robotaxi\/\">October 2 incident<\/a>\u00a0that saw a pedestrian \u2014 who had been initially hit by a human-driven car and landed in the path of a Cruise robotaxi \u2014 run over and dragged 20 feet by the AV. A video, which TechCrunch viewed a day after the incident, showed the robotaxi braking aggressively and coming to a stop over the woman. The DMV\u2019s order of suspension stated that Cruise withheld about seven seconds of video footage, which showed the robotaxi then attempting to pull over and subsequently dragging the woman 20 feet.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>Cruise, which had already faced increasing opposition from city officials in San Francisco, soon found itself hamstrung by investigations and pressure to stop operations. Without commercial permits to operate in San Francisco and an internal decision to\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/26\/cruise-pauses-all-driverless-robotaxi-operations-to-rebuild-public-trust\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/26\/cruise-pauses-all-driverless-robotaxi-operations-to-rebuild-public-trust\/\">pause its driverless fleets<\/a>\u00a0in other states, the company\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/cruise-begins-layoffs-starting-with-workers-who-supported-driverless-operations\/#:~:text=Cruise%20co%2Dfounder%20and%20CEO,many%20contingent%20workers%20it%20employed.\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/cruise-begins-layoffs-starting-with-workers-who-supported-driverless-operations\/\">laid off contract workers<\/a>, further deepening the malaise.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Cruise executives are taking a measured business approach that preserves cash and improves safety culture in an attempt to put GM&#8217;s troubled autonomous vehicle subsidiary on the right path. The first steps in this rebuilding plan, which includes pausing production on its Origin robotaxi, were laid out in an internal email sent to employees by [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2632790,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"10134eab-b7aa-31a3-9ba4-d1253ebe713e","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-22T23:51:33Z","apple_news_api_id":"fdd323e1-ccda-4c43-b59d-122d6666f472","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-22T23:51:52Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/A_dMj4czaTEO1nRItZmb0cg","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[1452,576654085],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- /techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=576654085"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=576654085"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=576654085"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=576654085"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2632253,"date":"2023-11-21T10:54:07","date_gmt":"2023-11-21T18:54:07","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2632253"},"modified":"2023-11-21T11:54:44","modified_gmt":"2023-11-21T19:54:44","slug":"tech-ceo-austin-russells-bid-to-buy-forbes-fails","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/21\/tech-ceo-austin-russells-bid-to-buy-forbes-fails\/","title":{"rendered":"Tech CEO Austin Russell&#8217;s bid to buy Forbes fails"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Luminar founder and CEO Austin Russell&#8217;s bid to buy Forbes Global Media Holdings has ended. Integrated Whale Media Investments, the Hong Kong-based parent company of Forbes, terminated the agreement with Russell after he failed to secure the ideal group of investors needed to close the deal.<\/p>\n<p>Bloomberg was the <a href=\"https:\/\/www.bloomberg.com\/news\/articles\/2023-11-21\/forbes-media-cancels-sale-to-tech-tycoon-after-missed-deadline?embedded-checkout=true&amp;sref=gni836kR\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">first to report<\/a> the news, citing an internal memo by Forbes Chief Executive Officer Mike Federle.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;The Forbes acquisition was all about impact, and to further the philosophy of business-for-good and philanthropy with the next generation of capitalism,&#8221; a statement provided by Austin Russell&#8217;s family office reads. &#8220;At this juncture, it was determined that it was in the best interest of the parties that the contract be terminated. We wish nothing but the best to the Forbes team.\u201d<\/p>\n<p>Russell, the 28-year-old founder and tech star, announced back in May plans to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/05\/12\/austin-russell-became-the-youngest-self-made-billionaire-in-2021-now-he-owns-forbes\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">buy an 82% stake<\/a> in Forbes Global Media Holdings in a deal that values the company at nearly $800 million. The intention, which was initially successful, was to bring in a number of investors to back the acquisition. The deal was supposed to close November 1, but fell apart after Indian investment firm Sun Group and others didn&#8217;t wire the money they were contractually obligated to, Axios <a href=\"https:\/\/www.axios.com\/2023\/11\/21\/forbes-deal-austin-russell-not-closed\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">reported earlier this month<\/a>. Sun Group vice chair Shiv Khemka had reportedly committed to invest $300 million into the acquisition.<\/p>\n<p>Russell was granted an extension to the deadline and has spent the past two weeks shoring up the multimillion-dollar shortfall. In another wrinkle, an updated agreement sent to the Russell camp contained terms to the investor group that were described as untenable, according to sources familiar with the deal.<\/p>\n<p>Forbes, a media company perhaps best known for its magazine and lists that rank billionaires, sold 95% of the company to Integrated Whale Media back in 2014. Forbes Media had planned to go public via a merger with special purpose acquisition company Magnum Opus Acquisition Limited, but called off the agreement in June 2022.<\/p>\n<p>Forbes has been up for sale ever since. Russell \u2014 who was described by Forbes itself in 2021 as the\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/www.forbes.com\/austin-russell-youngest-self-made-billionaire-2021\/#57a971d91fea\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/www.forbes.com\/austin-russell-youngest-self-made-billionaire-2021\/#57a971d91fea\">world\u2019s youngest self-made billionaire<\/a>\u00a0 \u2014 was the latest example of a tech magnate getting into the media business. Amazon founder Jeff Bezos bought The Washington Post in 2013 and Salesforce chair, CEO and co-founder Marc Benioff, along with his wife Lynne Benioff, acquired Time from parent company Meredith Corporation for $190 million in cash in 2018. More recently, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/10\/27\/elon-musk-bought-twitter\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Elon Musk bought Twitter<\/a> in a tumultuous deal that has resulted in the social media site&#8217;s name change to X.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Luminar founder and CEO Austin Russell&#8217;s bid to buy Forbes Global Media Holdings has ended. Integrated Whale Media Investments, the Hong Kong-based parent company of Forbes, terminated the agreement with Russell after he failed to secure the ideal group of investors needed to close the deal. Bloomberg was the first to report the news, citing [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2632270,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"2ccc9096-7344-363d-8956-e106f21b0d75","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-21T18:54:14Z","apple_news_api_id":"eaf1847f-0fd6-4337-be67-b8be6a45403a","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-21T19:54:48Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABA==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/A6vGEfw_WQze-Z7i-akVAOg","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[577030456],"tags":[449560553,449560555],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- /techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=449560555"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=449560555"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=449560555"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=449560555"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2632089,"date":"2023-11-21T08:38:29","date_gmt":"2023-11-21T16:38:29","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2632089"},"modified":"2023-11-21T11:34:56","modified_gmt":"2023-11-21T19:34:56","slug":"ford-downsizes-ev-battery-factory-plan","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/21\/ford-downsizes-ev-battery-factory-plan\/","title":{"rendered":"Ford downsizes EV battery factory plan"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Ford said Tuesday it will downsize its plans to build a factory in Michigan that makes cheaper lithium iron phosphate batteries using tech licensed from China\u2019s CATL. The decision comes two months after Ford <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/09\/25\/ford-halts-work-on-3-5b-ev-battery-factory-with-chinas-catl\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">paused the factory project<\/a>, known as the BlueOval Battery Park Michigan in Marshall; it&#8217;s also in line with the company&#8217;s decision to delay\u00a0about <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/26\/ford-delays-12b-in-ev-investments-as-automaker-wrestles-with-demand\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">$12 billion in planned investments on EVs<\/a>, including construction of a second battery plant with joint venture partner SK On.<\/p>\n<p>Ford, along with other automakers, have adjusted production timelines and factory plans as the rate of growth in EV sales has slowed and consumers have become more price sensitive.<\/p>\n<p>The scaled-down version of the EV battery factory will have a production capacity of 20 gigawatt-hours, reflecting about a 43% reduction. The factory, which is still slated to start battery cell production in 2026, will now employ an estimated 1,700 people, down from 2,500 jobs. Using back-of-the envelope math based on the capacity reduction, Ford will still need to invest about $2 billion in the project.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;While we remain bullish on our long-term strategy for electric vehicles, we are re-timing and resizing some investments. As stated previously, we have been evaluating BlueOval Battery Park Michigan in Marshall,&#8221; the company said in a statement Tuesday.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Ford said Tuesday it will downsize its plans to build a factory in Michigan that makes cheaper lithium iron phosphate batteries using tech licensed from China\u2019s CATL. The decision comes two months after Ford paused the factory project, known as the BlueOval Battery Park Michigan in Marshall; it&#8217;s also in line with the company&#8217;s decision [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2623239,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"1608f1b7-bd5a-33ce-82fd-cc7733965ce1","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-21T16:38:36Z","apple_news_api_id":"aa3c5feb-b5f8-4426-b43e-722a6f815e58","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-21T19:34:49Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/Aqjxf67X4RCa0PnIqb4FeWA","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[197443,76801,577177712,19071],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- nch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=19071"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=19071"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=19071"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=19071"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2631784,"date":"2023-11-20T13:05:34","date_gmt":"2023-11-20T21:05:34","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2631784"},"modified":"2023-11-20T16:30:08","modified_gmt":"2023-11-21T00:30:08","slug":"rivian-ceo-rj-scaringe-is-now-taking-over-the-top-product-role-at-the-ev-maker","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/20\/rivian-ceo-rj-scaringe-is-now-taking-over-the-top-product-role-at-the-ev-maker\/","title":{"rendered":"Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe is now taking over the top product role at the EV maker"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Rivian founder and CEO RJ Scaringe has taken on another role within the publicly traded EV maker as chief product officer Nick Kalayjian moves out of the job and eventually into an advisory position, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.sec.gov\/ix?doc=\/Archives\/edgar\/data\/0001874178\/000187417823000063\/rivn-20231120.htm\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">according to a regulatory filing<\/a> and internal emails viewed by TechCrunch. Scaringe will now be responsible for all product development \u2014 a critical position at a company working on a next-generation platform that is supposed to deliver more affordable EVs and help catapult Rivian into the mass market.<\/p>\n<p>Kalayjian will move to the role of executive vice president of vehicle engineering and propulsion and will continue to report to the CEO. Scaringe said in an an internal email viewed by TechCrunch that Kalayjian will be focused on &#8220;Peregrine&#8221; (the next-gen platform that will be used across all vehicles &#8212; R1, R2 and the electric delivery van) before he ultimately transfers to a product and technology advisory role.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;You have heard me repeatedly state in our company updates, it is critical that quality plays a central role across our business &#8212; this not only drives how our brand is perceived but it is a major enabler for our continued ramp,&#8221; Scaringe wrote in his email to employees.<\/p>\n<p>In a separate internal email, Kalayjian wrote:<\/p>\n<blockquote><p>With this, the time has come for me to make a change. Over the last few months, I\u2019ve taken time to Zoom Out and have been talking with RJ to understand how my involvement could continue to allow me to have an impact while letting me step back from my existing role. My desire to make changes aligns perfectly with RJ\u2019s desire to redirect more of his time and energy toward Product leadership. I have never met anyone in my life or career who is a better mix of engineer and visionary product leader than RJ. I know that him spending more time working with our Product teams will create significant value for all of us and our shareholders.<\/p><\/blockquote>\n<p>The move includes some other shuffling on the executive team, specifically on who reports to Scaringe and Kalayjian.<\/p>\n<p>Richard Farquhar, who has been vice president of propulsion, will be stepping away from his current role December 11 and into a new role as senior vice president of future R&amp;D. He will report to Scaringe upon his return next year. Quality and reliability teams led by Brad Thacker and Georgios Sarakakis will also report directly to Scaringe.<u><\/u><u><\/u><\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>Paul Frey, who heads up the charging, energy and adventure products, will now be responsible for battery as well, and will continue to report to Kalayjian. Silva Hiti, senior director of electric power conversion, and Henry Huang, senior director of power and thermal engineering, will also report directly to Kalayjian.<u><\/u><\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Rivian founder and CEO RJ Scaringe has taken on another role within the publicly traded EV maker as chief product officer Nick Kalayjian moves out of the job and eventually into an advisory position, according to a regulatory filing and internal emails viewed by TechCrunch. Scaringe will now be responsible for all product development \u2014 [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2428088,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"7a66a2c3-bfe7-36c5-8e7e-0d23116057dc","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-20T21:05:39Z","apple_news_api_id":"ba3ff384-514c-44e5-bb4e-39d91baa2d28","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-21T00:30:01Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABQ==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/Auj_zhFFMROW7TjnZG6otKA","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[197443,76801,576617966,576788909],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- name=search_term_string"}],"inLanguage":"en-US"},{"@type":"Organization","@id":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/#organization","name":"TechCrunch","alternateName":"TC","url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/","logo":{"@type":"ImageObject","inLanguage":"en-US","@id":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/#\/schema\/logo\/image\/","url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2018\/04\/tc-logo-2018-square-reverse2x.png?resize=1200,1200","contentUrl":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2018\/04\/tc-logo-2018-square-reverse2x.png?resize=1200,1200","width":1200,"height":1200,"caption":"TechCrunch"},"image":{"@id":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/#\/schema\/logo\/image\/"},"sameAs":["http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/www.facebook.com\/techcrunch","http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/twitter.com\/TechCrunch","http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/mstdn.social\/@TechCrunch","http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/bsky.app\/profile\/techcrunch.bsky.social","http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/www.threads.net\/@techcrunch"]}]}},"_links":{"self":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tags\/576788909"}],"collection":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tags"}],"about":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/taxonomies\/post_tag"}],"wp:post_type":[{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/posts?tags=576788909"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=576788909"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=576788909"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=576788909"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=576788909"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2631269,"date":"2023-11-19T18:22:52","date_gmt":"2023-11-20T02:22:52","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2631269"},"modified":"2023-12-01T12:18:46","modified_gmt":"2023-12-01T20:18:46","slug":"cruise-co-founder-and-ceo-kyle-vogt-resigns","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/19\/cruise-co-founder-and-ceo-kyle-vogt-resigns\/","title":{"rendered":"Cruise co-founder and CEO Kyle Vogt resigns"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Kyle Vogt, the serial entrepreneur who co-founded and led Cruise from a startup in a garage through its <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2016\/03\/11\/gm-buys-self-driving-tech-startup-cruise-as-part-of-a-plan-to-make-driverless-cars\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">acquisition<\/a> and ownership by General Motors, has resigned, according to an email sent to employees Sunday evening that TechCrunch has viewed.<\/p>\n<p>Dan Kan, who co-founded Cruise with Vogt and held a far less public-facing role, has also resigned, TechCrunch has confirmed with sources familiar with the matter.<\/p>\n<p>In a separate internal email, also viewed by TechCrunch, GM Chair and CEO Mary Barra announced that Mo Elshenawy, who is executive vice president of engineering at Cruise, will serve as president and CTO for Cruise. Craig Glidden, a Cruise board member and GM\u2019s EVP of legal and policy who was <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/gm-inserts-exec-at-cruise-as-safety-review-expands-manual-self-driving-paused\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">recently put in charge<\/a>\u00a0as chief administrative officer at Cruise, will continue in that role. Jon McNeill, a member of GM\u2019s board, has been appointed vice chairman of the Cruise board. McNeill, who joined the Cruise board recently and was previously chief operating officer at Lyft and president of Tesla, will now serve alongside Cruise Board Chair Mary Barra. A statement from a Cruise spokesperson confirms Barra&#8217;s email.<\/p>\n<p>As of Sunday, no one had been named to the CEO spot.<\/p>\n<p><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">The executive shakeup comes less than a month after the California Department of Motor Vehicles <\/span><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/24\/dmv-immediately-suspends-cruises-robotaxi-permit-in-california\/\"><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">suspended Cruise\u2019s permits<\/span><\/a><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\"> to operate self-driving vehicles on public roads after an <\/span><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/03\/in-latest-cruise-incident-video-shows-pedestrian-struck-by-human-driven-car-then-run-over-by-robotaxi\/\"><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">October 2 incident<\/span><\/a><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\"> that saw a pedestrian &#8212; who had been initially hit by a human-driven car and landed in the path of a Cruise robotaxi &#8212; run over and dragged 20 feet by the AV. A video, which TechCrunch viewed a day after the incident, showed the robotaxi braking aggressively and coming to a stop over the woman. The DMV\u2019s order of suspension stated that Cruise withheld about seven seconds of video footage, which showed the robotaxi then attempting to pull over and subsequently dragging the woman 20 feet.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>\n<p>Vogt&#8217;s email sent to all employees \u2014 and viewed by TechCrunch \u2014 reads:<\/p>\n<blockquote><p>I have resigned from my position as CEO of Cruise.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>The last 10 years have been amazing, and I&#8217;m grateful to everyone who helped Cruise along the way. The startup I launched in my garage has given over 250,000 driverless rides across several cities, with each ride inspiring people with a small taste of the future.<\/p>\n<p>Cruise is still just getting started, and I believe it has a great future ahead. You all are brilliant, driven and resilient. I&#8217;m deeply saddened I won&#8217;t be working next to you anymore. However, I know you&#8217;re executing against a very strong, multi-year technology roadmap and exciting product vision, and I&#8217;m thrilled to see what Cruise has in store in its next chapter!<\/p>\n<p>Cruisers, you&#8217;ve got this! Regardless of what originally brought you to work on AVs, remember why this work matters. The status quo on our roads sucks, but together we&#8217;ve proven there is something far better around the corner.<\/p><\/blockquote>\n<p>Vogt also posted a message Sunday evening on the social media site X that used similar language as the internal email. He ended the social media thread <a href=\"https:\/\/x.com\/kvogt\/status\/1726428105873764714?s=20\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">with this message<\/a>: &#8220;As for what\u2019s next for me, I plan to spend time with my family and explore some new ideas. Thanks for the great ride!&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>Barra&#8217;s internal email, which was sent about 15 minutes after Vogt sent his, thanked him for this &#8220;tremendous vision, passion and dedication over the past decade.&#8221; The emailed continued:<\/p>\n<blockquote><p>The Cruise Board understands and respects his decision to resign as CEO, and we wish him well in his next chapter. We continue to believe strongly in Cruise&#8217;s mission and the potential of its transformative technology as we look to make transportation safer, cleaner and more accessible.<\/p><\/blockquote>\n<p>Barra later emphasized that &#8220;the board and I also want you to know that we are intensely focused on setting Cruise up for long-term success. Public trust is essential to this. As we work to rebuild that trust, safety, transparency and accountability will be our north stars.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">Morale at Cruise has been low since the October 2 incident, with employees pointing the finger at poor management that didn\u2019t prioritize safety at the company. Without commercial permits to operate in San Francisco and an internal decision to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/26\/cruise-pauses-all-driverless-robotaxi-operations-to-rebuild-public-trust\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">pause its driverless fleets<\/a> in other states, the company <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/cruise-begins-layoffs-starting-with-workers-who-supported-driverless-operations\/#:~:text=Cruise%20co%2Dfounder%20and%20CEO,many%20contingent%20workers%20it%20employed.\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">laid off contract workers<\/a>, further deepening the malaise.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>\n<p>The initial layoffs included contract workers who had jobs cleaning, charging and maintaining the vehicles as well as answering customer support inquiries. Not all contingent workers, who are employed by a third party, were laid off. However, more layoffs are expected at the company that employs\u00a0about 4,000 full-time employees.<\/p>\n<p><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">Employee discontent was further inflamed last week when <\/span><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/cruise-suspends-employee-stock-program-corp-bonuses-moved-up\/\"><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">Cruise suspended its employee share-selling program <\/span><\/a><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">for the fourth quarter. Sources who spoke to TechCrunch on the condition of anonymity said they could lose upwards of tens of thousands of dollars as a result of this decision.<\/span><\/p>\n<p><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">Over the weekend, Cruise backtracked on that move. Vogt sent out an email Saturday saying that certain employees could sell a limited number of shares in a one-time opportunity. Vogt didn\u2019t provide many details but said the company was developing a plan to conduct a new tender offer to provide restricted stock unit liquidity to mitigate potential tax implications. TechCrunch has viewed the email.<\/span><\/p>\n<p><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">Vogt went on to offer his staff a blanket apology for \u201cthe situation Cruise is in today.\u201d<\/span><\/p>\n<p><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">Vogt and Kan, who more recently held the chief product officer role, founded the autonomous vehicle company in 2013. Initially, the pair had focused on kits that could retrofit a vehicle and turn it into a self-driving car. The startup soon pivoted to a different business model. GM took interest and <a href=\"https:\/\/fortune.com\/2016\/03\/11\/gm-buying-self-driving-tech-startup-for-more-than-1-billion\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">acquired the company<\/a> in March 2016 in a deal of cash and stock valued at more than $1 billion.\u00a0<\/span><\/p>\n<p><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">Previously, Vogt had co-founded Justin.tv, a website that allowed anyone to broadcast video online; Twitch, a live-streaming platform; and Socialcam, a mobile social video app. <\/span><span style=\"font-weight: 400;\">Twitch was acquired by Amazon in 2014 for $970 million, and Socialcam by Autodesk for $60 million in 2012.<\/span><\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Kyle Vogt, the serial entrepreneur who co-founded and led Cruise from a startup in a garage through its acquisition and ownership by General Motors, has resigned, according to an email sent to employees Sunday evening that TechCrunch has viewed. Dan Kan, who co-founded Cruise with Vogt and held a far less public-facing role, has also [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2604221,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"9bedb18f-83d0-3c2d-91cb-902c83c3132b","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-20T02:26:57Z","apple_news_api_id":"19ba391f-8bc9-4cd4-bf45-88ff791df4e2","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-20T19:24:50Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAEg==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AGbo5H4vJTNS_RYj_eR304g","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[1452,22376,449341376,104747],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- runch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=104747"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=104747"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=104747"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=104747"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2631262,"date":"2023-11-19T15:18:06","date_gmt":"2023-11-19T23:18:06","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2631262"},"modified":"2023-11-20T11:45:14","modified_gmt":"2023-11-20T19:45:14","slug":"cruises-mea-culpa-and-everything-that-stood-out-at-the-la-auto-show","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/19\/cruises-mea-culpa-and-everything-that-stood-out-at-the-la-auto-show\/","title":{"rendered":"Cruise&#8217;s mea culpa and everything that stood out at the LA Auto Show"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\"><em>The Station is a weekly newsletter dedicated to all things transportation.\u00a0Sign up here \u2014\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/newsletters\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/newsletters\">just click The Station<\/a>\u00a0\u2014 to receive the newsletter every weekend in your inbox. Subscribe for free.<\/em><\/p>\n<p>Welcome back to The Station, your central hub for all past, present and future means of moving people and packages from Point A to Point B.<\/p>\n<p>The <strong>LA Auto Show<\/strong> was this week and while it no longer has the cache of pre-COVID days, there was still some notable news that came out of the event. TechCrunch reporter Harri Weber was on the scene helping me cover the news. One of the biggest announcements had nothing to do with a vehicle reveal. Nope, it was <strong>Amazon<\/strong> declaring it was now going to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/amazon-to-sell-cars-online-starting-with-hyundai\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">sell cars online<\/a>, starting with <strong>Hyundai<\/strong>.<\/p>\n<p>Other coverage included the <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/lucid-gravity-suv-ev\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Lucid Gravity SUV debut<\/a> and a handy <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/17\/la-auto-show-the-tech-evs-and-cars-that-got-our-attention\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">roundup<\/a> that covered the tech, EVs and other fun stuff that got our attention.<\/p>\n<p><em>Alrighty, let&#8217;s dive in!<\/em><\/p>\n<hr \/>\n<p><em>Want to reach out with a tip, comment or complaint? Email Kirsten at <a href=\"mailto:kirsten.korosec@techcrunch.com\">kirsten.korosec@techcrunch.com<\/a>.<\/em><\/p>\n<p><em>Reminder: You can drop us a note at <a href=\"mailto:tips@techcrunch.com\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">tips@techcrunch.com<\/a>.\u00a0<strong>If you prefer to remain anonymous<\/strong>,\u00a0<\/em><a href=\"https:\/\/link.techcrunch.com\/click\/30325329.4639\/aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS9nb3QtYS10aXAvP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09VENuZXdzbGV0dGVyJnRwY2M9VEN0cmFuc3BvcnRhdGlvbm5ld3NsZXR0ZXI\/606b5c505a5ea2367772122aBd61e79c3\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><em>click here to contact us<\/em><\/a><em>, which includes SecureDrop (<\/em><a href=\"https:\/\/link.techcrunch.com\/click\/30325329.4639\/aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS9zZWN1cmVkcm9wP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09VENuZXdzbGV0dGVyJnRwY2M9VEN0cmFuc3BvcnRhdGlvbm5ld3NsZXR0ZXI\/606b5c505a5ea2367772122aB80a9346e\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><em>instructions here<\/em><\/a><em>) and various encrypted messaging apps.<\/em><\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<h2>Micromobbin&#8217;<\/h2>\n<p><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png\"><img fetchpriority=\"high\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone wp-image-1894192 size-full\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png\" alt=\"the station scooter1a\" width=\"1600\" height=\"401\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png 1600w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=150,38 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=300,75 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=768,192 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=680,170 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=1536,385 1536w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=1200,301 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=50,13 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1600px) 100vw, 1600px\" \/><\/a><\/p>\n<p><strong>Upway<\/strong>, the French refurbished e-bike marketplace, has <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/refurbished-e-bike-marketplace-upway-raised-30m-at-a-higher-valuation\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">raised $30 million<\/a> at an increased valuation. Normally this piece of news would go in our deals section below, but I wanted to ponder on the meaning of this up round.<\/p>\n<p>Upway is present in multiple countries in Europe and recently expanded to the U.S. It takes in used e-bikes from reputable and desirable brands, fixes them up and sends them out to customers at a decent price. Getting this business right requires smart supply chain and logistics management, and it appears Upway is on the right path. In today&#8217;s capital markets, it&#8217;s not as easy to get new investors if you&#8217;re not proving out business sustainability.<\/p>\n<p>Zooming out to the macro environment, e-bikes aren&#8217;t going anywhere. In fact, as more people buy new bikes, the market for used ones grows. And with all the e-bike battery fires we&#8217;ve seen, a company offering safely refurbished e-bikes seems like a pretty good deal.<\/p>\n<p style=\"text-align: right;\"><em>\u2014 Rebecca Bellan<\/em><\/p>\n<h2>Deal of the week<\/h2>\n<p><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png\"><img decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone wp-image-1896226 size-full\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png\" alt=\"money the station\" width=\"1024\" height=\"256\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png 1024w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=150,38 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=300,75 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=768,192 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=680,170 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=50,13 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><\/a><\/p>\n<p>Just a roundup this week!<\/p>\n<p><strong>Divergent Technologies<\/strong>, a Torrance, California-based startup that developed an industrial digital manufacturing system, raised $230 million in a Series D funding round led by a $100 million investment from Hexagon AB. The round also included participation from new and existing institutional and family office investors.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Gravity<\/strong>, an EV infrastructure startup, raised $13 million in a seed round led by GV (Google Ventures).<\/p>\n<p><strong>GM<\/strong> acquired key Tesla gigacasting supplier Tooling &amp; Equipment International, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.reuters.com\/business\/autos-transportation\/gm-snatches-key-tesla-gigacasting-supplier-2023-11-15\/#_ga=2.147748791.1012943215.1700413261-2045908248.1698794971\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Reuters reported<\/a>.<\/p>\n<p><strong>InDrive<\/strong>, the &#8220;bid-based&#8221; ride-hail platform popular in Latin America and Asia, launched a <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/indrive-launches-ventures-and-ma-arm-to-invest-100m-in-startups-across-emerging-markets\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">venture and merger and acquisition division<\/a> named New Ventures to invest up to $100 million in startups within emerging markets over the next few years. The $100 million won&#8217;t come from a fund, exactly. The plan is to invest the money &#8220;over the next few years by making annual allocations of investment capital from our balance sheet, starting in 2024,&#8221; Andries Smit, vice president of New Ventures told TechCrunch.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Luup<\/strong>, a Tokyo-based shared e-scooter startup, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.japantimes.co.jp\/business\/2023\/11\/13\/companies\/japan-e-scooter-startup\/?utm_source=substack&amp;utm_medium=email\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">raised $24 million<\/a> to expand its charge ports to 10,000 by 2025, up from around 4,900 today.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Revolv<\/strong>, an electric commercial fleet company based in San Francisco, raised $25 million in equity project financing\u00a0 from Greenbacker Capital Management.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Shekel Mobility<\/strong>, a B2B auto dealers marketplace in Africa, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/13\/shekel-mobility-a-b2b-marketplace-for-auto-dealers-in-africa-raises-7m-led-by-ventures-platform-and-mac-vc\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">raised more than $7 million<\/a> in funding that was comprised of $3.2 million in equity and over $4 million in debt. Ventures Platform co-led the round alongside MaC Venture Capital. Other investors include Y Combinator, Rebel Fund, Unpopular Ventures, Maiora Capital, PageOne Lab Inc., Phoenix Investment Club, Heirloom VC, Pioneer Ventures and other angel investors. Meanwhile, Zedvance, VFD Microfinance Bank, Zenith Bank and Fluna, amongst others, provided the debt component.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Tenet<\/strong>, an EV financing startup based in New York, <a href=\"https:\/\/www.axios.com\/pro\/climate-deals\/2023\/11\/15\/tenet-nyca-series-a-ev-loans\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">raised $10 million<\/a> in a Series A funding round led by Nyca Partners. Assurant Ventures and Giant Ventures also participated.<\/p>\n<h2>Notable reads and other tidbits<\/h2>\n<h3><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png\"><img decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone wp-image-1926823 size-full\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"1024\" height=\"257\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png 1024w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=150,38 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=300,75 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=768,193 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=680,171 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=50,13 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><\/a><\/h3>\n<h3>Autonomous vehicles<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Texas<\/strong> is poised to be the next hotbed for autonomous vehicles and a likely regulatory battleground. AVs aren&#8217;t regulated in the state, but as robotaxi incidents rise, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/the-wild-west-of-robotaxis-will-texas-be-a-regulatory-haven-or-chaos-unleashed\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">cities may turn to one weapon<\/a> that California doesn&#8217;t have, TechCrunch reporter Rebecca Bellan writes.<\/p>\n<p>Meanwhile, GM has taken the wheel at its troubled AV subsidiary Cruise, going as far as <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/gm-inserts-exec-at-cruise-as-safety-review-expands-manual-self-driving-paused\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">inserting one of its own executives<\/a>\u00a0to head up the self-driving car company&#8217;s legal and policy, communications and finance teams.<\/p>\n<p>A number of <strong>Cruise<\/strong> employees have reported to TechCrunch that morale is at a new low point thanks to a decision by parent company GM to<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/cruise-suspends-employee-stock-program-corp-bonuses-moved-up\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"> suspend its employee share-selling program<\/a>. But wait! On Saturday, Cruise co-founder and CEO <strong>Kyle Vogt<\/strong> sent an email to employees (which TechCrunch has seen) apologizing to employees, taking responsibility and reversing course, sort of, on the share sale. He opened with:<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;I know the news about changes to our RLO program was extemely difficult to hear, and that there were many unanswered questions. We left you with concerns about your colleagues, your careers, and your personal finances. That&#8217;s the last thing I want for anyone, and I am truly sorry.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>Vogt went on to say that the company was working on a way to conduct a new tender offer that would mitigate potential tax obligations.<\/p>\n<p>He later wrote that &#8220;as CEO, I take responsibility for the situation Cruise is in today. There are no excuses, and there is no sugar coating what happened.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p><strong>Update:<\/strong> On Sunday evening, and after The Station was sent to subscribers, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/19\/cruise-co-founder-and-ceo-kyle-vogt-resigns\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Vogt resigned<\/a> from his CEO position.<\/p>\n<h3>Earnings<\/h3>\n<p>It was a slower earnings week for transportation. Still, there were a couple of noteworthy news items.<\/p>\n<p>It&#8217;s still not looking good for <strong>Bird<\/strong>,\u00a0which <a href=\"https:\/\/ir.bird.co\/financial-information\/financial-results\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">closed out the quarter<\/a> after getting delisted from the stock market with a $19.8 million loss. Bird&#8217;s going concern warning is very much still in effect &#8212; the company has incurred recurring losses and negative cash flows since its inception and has accumulated a deficit of $1.6 billion as of September 30, 2023.<\/p>\n<p>Revenue remains light, despite the Spin buy, at $54.3 million (down from $72.8 million in Q3 2022). Now we&#8217;re coming into winter, when cash flows will drop even more significantly. Bird closed out the quarter with $10.2 million in cash and cash equivalents, which by my calculations gives it another nine months with its current cash burn. The company says it won&#8217;t be able to meet obligations over the next 12 months.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Gogoro<\/strong>, too, is still feeling its previous stings of <a href=\"https:\/\/www.gogoro.com\/news\/q3-2023-earnings\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">currency conversions<\/a>. Its revenue of $91.8 million for the quarter is down 10.2% YoY. However, battery swapping service revenue of $33.6 million is up 10.4% YoY. The battery swapping giant closed Q3 with a net loss of $3.1 million, down from a net income of $56.4 million in the same quarter last year. On an adjusted basis, Gogoro reported EBITDA of $13.1 million, which is up from $9.2 million in Q3 2022.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Fisker<\/strong> also had a troubling third quarter, reporting a wider-than-expected loss.<\/p>\n<h3>Electric vehicles, charging &amp; batteries<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Arrival<\/strong>, the once buzzy EV startup that went public via a merger with a blank check company, secured a <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/15\/arrival-secures-emergency-cash-so-it-can-sell-itself\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">$50 million bridge loan<\/a> \u2014 funds that will keep it afloat long enough to explore a potential sale.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Cadillac<\/strong> <a href=\"https:\/\/www.cadillac.com\/upcoming-vehicles\/optiq\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">debuted the Optiq<\/a>, an entry-level compact crossover EV that will slot below the Lyriq.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Candela&#8217;s<\/strong> <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/candela-p-12-electric-hydrofoiling-ferry-takes-flight\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">electric hydrofoiling ferry<\/a>, which is now in production, took its first \u201cflight\u201d in Stockholm.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Exxon<\/strong> wants to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/13\/exxon-wants-to-drill-enough-lithium-out-of-arkansas-to-power-1m-evs-per-year\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">drill enough lithium<\/a> out of Arkansas to power 1 million EVs per year.<\/p>\n<p><strong>GM<\/strong> <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/gm-absorbs-commercial-ev-unit-brightdrop-ceo-is-out\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">absorbed<\/a> <strong>BrightDrop<\/strong>, the wholly owned commercial EV subsidiary, a change that included ousting its CEO. Does the same fate await Cruise, another one of its subsidiaries?<\/p>\n<p><strong>Mercedes<\/strong> plans to build around <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/buc-ees-mercedes-partnership\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">30 fast-charging hubs<\/a> at Buc-ee&#8217;s locations throughout the South. TC+ reporter Tim de Chant digs into why supersize convenience stores might be key to unlocking EV fast charging.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Redwood Materials<\/strong> will <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/toyota-turning-to-redwood-materials-critical-battery-materials-us-factory\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">supply Toyota<\/a> with cathode material and anode copper foil for battery cells produced at the automaker&#8217;s $13.9 billion factory in North Carolina that&#8217;s slated to go into production in 2025.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Tesla<\/strong> hits reverse on its previous threat to sue <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/tesla-hits-reverse-on-threat-to-sue-cybertruck-resellers\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Cybertruck resellers<\/a>. Also, an upgraded <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/upgraded-tesla-cyberquad-for-kids-back-on-sale-after-safety-recall\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Tesla Cyberquad for kids<\/a> is back on sale after a safety recall.<\/p>\n<h3>Future of flight<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Archer Aviation<\/strong> signed a memorandum of understanding with Air Chateau, a private aviation operator in UAE, at a value of around $500 million for the planned purchase of up to 100 of Archer&#8217;s Midnight eVTOLs.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Joby Aviation<\/strong> and Volocopter gave the public a vivid glimpse of what the <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/13\/joby-volocopter-fly-electric-air-taxis-over-new-york-city\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">future of aviation<\/a> might look like, with both companies performing brief demonstration flights of their electric aircraft in New York City.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Zipline<\/strong> hit an important milestone in its U.S. operations. The Federal Aviation Administration approved Zipline for flying its autonomous drone without line of sight visual observers. Soon after the <a href=\"https:\/\/www.flyzipline.com\/newsroom\/stories\/articles\/aviation-history-faa-drone-delivery-at-scale-Zipline\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">FAA lifted this requirement<\/a>, Zipline flew its Platform 1 aircraft in the Salt Lake City area without the previously required visual observers. Zipline is now flying in Utah without this requirement and will soon expand that approach across its U.S. operations, according to the company.<\/p>\n<h3>In-car tech<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Feds<\/strong> want <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/ntsb-speed-reduction-tech-in-every-new-car\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">speed reduction tech<\/a> in every new car. Are American drivers ready?<\/p>\n<p>A software update bricked <strong>Rivian<\/strong> infotainment systems. About <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/a-software-update-bricked-rivian-infotainment-systems\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">3% of its vehicles<\/a> were impacted by the issue, which is being addressed with an over-the-air software update.<\/p>\n<h3>Ride-hailing<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Uber<\/strong> is introducing new features geared toward addressing the issues of <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/13\/uber-takes-steps-to-combat-unfair-driver-deactivations\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">unfair deactivations<\/a> that ride-hail and delivery drivers often face.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Welcome back to The Station, your central hub for all past, present and future means of moving people and packages from Point A to Point B.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2630106,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"b6928cb8-6ad4-3c58-ab67-86c90b863962","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-19T23:18:16Z","apple_news_api_id":"b5e60dba-88ed-4e41-bc18-f8805eb2c680","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-20T19:45:06Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAg==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AteYNuojtTkG8GPiAXrLGgA","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[1452,76801,576717918],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!--
