/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=576717918"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=576717918"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=576717918"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=576717918"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2630427,"date":"2023-11-16T15:53:20","date_gmt":"2023-11-16T23:53:20","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2630427"},"modified":"2023-11-17T10:26:37","modified_gmt":"2023-11-17T18:26:37","slug":"gm-absorbs-commercial-ev-unit-brightdrop-ceo-is-out","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/gm-absorbs-commercial-ev-unit-brightdrop-ceo-is-out\/","title":{"rendered":"GM absorbs commercial EV unit BrightDrop, CEO is out"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">BrightDrop, the commercial EV subsidiary of General Motors that launched in 2021, is being swallowed up by its parent company.<\/p>\n<p>GM said Thursday that BrightDrop \u2014 a &#8220;startup&#8221; that spun out of the automaker&#8217;s Global Innovation organization to become a wholly owned subsidiary \u2014 will become part of GM. BrightDrop CEO Travis Katz, who was an entrepreneur-in-residence at Redpoint Ventures before taking the top post, has stepped down from the CEO role and will no longer be employed by GM, the company confirmed. BrightDrop CTO Anthony Armenta is staying on and will be joining the leadership team at GM&#8217;s software division, which is led by Mike Abbott, the former vice president of engineering for Apple\u2019s Cloud Services division, who joined in May.<\/p>\n<p>A small number of BrightDrop employees are expected to be impacted by the change as some positions are eliminated. The company said the majority of those employees should be able to move to other jobs within GM.<\/p>\n<p>GM has cast the move in a positive light, noting that absorbing BrightDrop into the company will benefit fleet customers by providing them with an efficient single point of contact through its new commercial brand GM Envolve. <a style=\"background-color: #ffffff; font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\" href=\"https:\/\/investor.gm.com\/news-releases\/news-release-details\/gm-envolve-leverages-total-power-gm-create-one-stop-shop\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">GM Envolve<\/a><span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\">, which launched in May 2023, encompasses the automaker&#8217;s entire commercial portfolio, including gas-powered and EV vehicles as well as connected services and software used by fleet customers. BrightDrop was created to sell an ecosystem of electric and connected products to commercial customers. Its first products were an electric van called the EV600 and a pod-like electric pallet dubbed EP1.<\/span><\/p>\n<p>&#8220;We are committed to helping our fleet customers drive their businesses forward,&#8221; Rory Harvey, EVP and president at GM North America said in an emailed statement. &#8220;Our innovative commercial solutions and EV vehicle options ranging from the Chevrolet Bolt EV to the BrightDrop Zevo are all available through GM Envolve. This gives our customers one stop shopping for all their commercial needs.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>The <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2021\/01\/12\/gm-targets-delivery-companies-with-new-ev-business-unit-brightdrop\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">idea for BrightDrop<\/a> was sparked by a team within Global Innovation \u2014 the same in-house organization that led to the creation of OnStar Insurance, OnStar Guardian and GM Defense \u2014\u00a0that was evaluating the growth of e-commerce and consumer demand for online delivery, which had been exacerbated by COVID-19.<\/p>\n<p>When it first launched, GM touted BrightDrop&#8217;s independence and startup-like structure as assets that would allow it to move quickly and capture market share. Months after it launched, Katz told TechCrunch that data harvested from these commercial EVs was where the company could build lasting relationships. For instance, the\u00a0logistics system can track the chain of custody, how trucks are traversing routes and how packages are moving to help determine if deliveries are hitting a bottleneck and how a company can eliminate inefficiencies. \u201cLong term, I think we see that\u2019s \u2026 the really exciting opportunity. We see ourselves as a solutions provider, but it\u2019s really with software at the core,\u201d <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2021\/06\/29\/gms-newest-startup-aims-squarely-at-the-commercial-ev-market\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Katz said back in 2021<\/a>.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>GM also had ambitious goals for BrightDrop, stating last November during its investor day that the subsidiary was on track to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/11\/17\/brightdrop-is-tracking-1-billion-revenue-in-2023\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">reach $1 billion in revenue in 2023<\/a>. The company\u00a0reported at the time that it has received more than 25,000 reservations and letters of intent from customers, including Walmart, Hertz and FedEx.<\/p>\n<p>It&#8217;s unclear if BrightDrop met that target as GM has not broken out its financials.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>BrightDrop, the commercial EV subsidiary of General Motors that launched in 2021, is being swallowed up by its parent company. GM said Thursday that BrightDrop \u2014 a &#8220;startup&#8221; that spun out of the automaker&#8217;s Global Innovation organization to become a wholly owned subsidiary \u2014 will become part of GM. BrightDrop CEO Travis Katz, who was [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2580075,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"c96ac299-b72d-3195-aa36-23928d8d2778","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-16T23:53:28Z","apple_news_api_id":"2804cfe7-fd55-480c-af95-22690c2690dc","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-17T18:26:15Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAw==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AKATP5_1VSAyvlSJpDCaQ3A","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[576844118,197443,76801,73527],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- This site is optimized with the Yoast SEO Premium plugin v20.2.1 (Yoast SEO v20.2.1) - https:\/\/yoast.com\/wordpress\/plugins\/seo\/ -->\n<title>GM absorbs commercial EV unit BrightDrop, CEO is out | TechCrunch<\/title>\n<meta name=\"description\" content=\"BrightDrop, the commercial EV subsidiary of GM that launched in 2021, is being swallowed up by its parent company. nch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=73527"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=73527"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=73527"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=73527"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2630246,"date":"2023-11-16T12:21:19","date_gmt":"2023-11-16T20:21:19","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2630246"},"modified":"2023-12-01T12:20:04","modified_gmt":"2023-12-01T20:20:04","slug":"cruise-suspends-employee-stock-program-corp-bonuses-moved-up","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/cruise-suspends-employee-stock-program-corp-bonuses-moved-up\/","title":{"rendered":"Cruise suspends employee stock program, corp bonuses moved up"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Cruise, the autonomous vehicle subsidiary of General Motors, told staff Thursday via email that the employee share-selling program for the fourth quarter is suspended, following an <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/03\/in-latest-cruise-incident-video-shows-pedestrian-struck-by-human-driven-car-then-run-over-by-robotaxi\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">incident<\/a> that resulted in the robotaxi company <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/24\/cpuc-pulls-cruise-robotaxi-permit-after-dmv-suspension\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">losing its permits<\/a> to operate in California. Cruise cited the need to reevaluate how to offer competitive compensation, according to sources who spoke to TechCrunch on the condition of anonymity.<\/p>\n<p>Cruise has confirmed the news to TechCrunch.<\/p>\n<p>The loss to employees is highly dependent on when they started and what the stock price was at that time. Some employees could be at a loss easily north of $100,000. Sources we spoke to indicated they would be losing upwards of tens of thousands of dollars.<\/p>\n<p>Corporate bonuses were also moved up two months, from March to January. Some sources suggest senior leadership adjusted the corporate bonus schedule to assuage workers who have described low morale throughout its ranks.<\/p>\n<p>To that point, Cruise issued a surprise company holiday for tomorrow to ostensibly boost morale among employees who have expressed disappointment in the plan. Sources also speculate that the holiday is a chance for executives to plan layoffs or operational changes.<\/p>\n<p>The employee share program involves GM buying back vested equity on a quarterly basis to facilitate recurring liquidity. The shares were historically bought based on valuation, but Cruise&#8217;s valuation has changed since an <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/03\/in-latest-cruise-incident-video-shows-pedestrian-struck-by-human-driven-car-then-run-over-by-robotaxi\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">October 2 incident<\/a> that left a pedestrian stuck under and dragged by a Cruise robotaxi. Canceling the program, sources say, makes the shares worthless.<\/p>\n<p>Cruise employees still hold shares in the company, but since these are not publicly traded they can only be sold if GM schedules a liquidity event, which has traditionally occurred every quarter. A third party evaluates and sets a valuation for the shares before there is a tender offer.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>After losing its <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/24\/dmv-immediately-suspends-cruises-robotaxi-permit-in-california\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">permits in California,<\/a> pausing all operations (<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/26\/cruise-pauses-all-driverless-robotaxi-operations-to-rebuild-public-trust\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">driverless<\/a> and <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/14\/gm-inserts-exec-at-cruise-as-safety-review-expands-manual-self-driving-paused\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">manual<\/a>) across the country, and pausing <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/06\/gm-halts-production-of-cruise-origin-robotaxi-amid-suspended-operations\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">production on the purpose-built Origin robotaxi<\/a>, layoffs are imminent. Cruise already started last week with <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/cruise-begins-layoffs-starting-with-workers-who-supported-driverless-operations\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">contract workers<\/a>.<\/p>\n<p class=\"text__text__1FZLe text__dark-grey__3Ml43 text__regular__2N1Xr text__small__1kGq2 body__full_width__ekUdw body__small_body__2vQyf article-body__paragraph__2-BtD\" data-testid=\"paragraph-5\">Cruise has lost more than $8 billion since 2017, including $728 million in the third quarter of 2023, according to GM financial records. Cruise closed the third quarter with $1.7 billion in cash, which should give it nine months of runway.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Cruise, the autonomous vehicle subsidiary of General Motors, told staff Thursday via email that the employee share-selling program for the fourth quarter is suspended, following an incident that resulted in the robotaxi company losing its permits to operate in California. Cruise cited the need to reevaluate how to offer competitive compensation, according to sources who [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574434,"featured_media":2604221,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"06879a7c-9a38-3e58-8f5e-5b750e630199","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-16T20:21:26Z","apple_news_api_id":"95a9cca6-ab04-4648-995e-4ebea3ff3f6e","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-17T00:24:20Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAw==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AlanMpqsERkiZXk6-o_8_bg","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[1452,22376,73527,104747],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- runch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=104747"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=104747"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=104747"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=104747"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2630073,"date":"2023-11-16T10:22:16","date_gmt":"2023-11-16T18:22:16","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2630073"},"modified":"2023-11-16T10:32:06","modified_gmt":"2023-11-16T18:32:06","slug":"amazon-to-sell-cars-online-starting-with-hyundai","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/amazon-to-sell-cars-online-starting-with-hyundai\/","title":{"rendered":"Amazon to sell cars online, starting with Hyundai"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">It was inevitable. Amazon, which got its start selling books, is getting into the car business.<\/p>\n<p>The e-commerce giant along with new partner Hyundai announced Thursday at the 2023 LA Auto Show that it will start selling vehicles on its website in the second half of 2024. Hyundai vehicles will be the first vehicles sold on Amazon.com&#8217;s U.S. store with other brands following later in the year.<\/p>\n<p>The Amazon car sales section will allow customers to shop for vehicles in their area based on a range of preferences, including model, trim, color, and features, choose their preferred car, and then check out online with their chosen payment and financing options. Customers will be able to buy a vehicle online and then pick it up or have it delivered by their local dealership, according to Amazon.<\/p>\n<p>Amazon already sells car accessories and operates an &#8220;Amazon Vehicle Showrooms&#8221; site that allows manufacturers to advertise. But until now, customers couldn&#8217;t actually buy that car, truck or SUV they were researching.<\/p>\n<p>Amazon says this shopping experience &#8220;will create another way for dealers to build awareness of their selection and offer convenience to their customers,&#8221; language that suggests dealerships are on board with this move. The structure still rewards dealerships, unlike the direct sales models that completely sidestep the business model. When vehicles go on sale at Amazon, the local Hyundai dealer will be the seller of record.<\/p>\n<p>Mike Sullivan, the president and owner of several LA-area dealerships, including the Hyundai Santa Monica location, publicly supported the news while on stage at the LA Auto Show.<\/p>\n<p>&#8220;We&#8217;re now partnering with one of the world&#8217;s leading digital retailers,&#8221; Sullivan said. &#8220;They&#8217;ve led the way in delivering customer convenience, and now they&#8217;re going to help us take our customer experience to the next level. Amazon also brings massive reach and marketing power to connect to more customers. We can&#8217;t wait to get started.&#8221;<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>Hyundai and Amazon are also partnering in other areas, the companies announced Thursday. Hyundai said it will use Amazon Web Services as its preferred cloud provider and will integrate Alexa voice assistant in the automaker&#8217;s next generation of vehicles starting in 2025.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>It was inevitable. Amazon, which got its start selling books, is getting into the car business. The e-commerce giant along with new partner Hyundai announced Thursday at the 2023 LA Auto Show that it will start selling vehicles on its website in the second half of 2024. Hyundai vehicles will be the first vehicles sold [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2630131,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"477e39b1-6f33-38fa-be64-a029552ff03c","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-16T18:22:24Z","apple_news_api_id":"7f389414-a890-4cb4-9955-31dd3e542522","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-16T18:32:12Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAg==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AfziUFKiQTLSZVTHdPlQlIg","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- This site is optimized with the Yoast SEO Premium plugin v20.2.1 (Yoast SEO v20.2.1) - https:\/\/yoast.com\/wordpress\/plugins\/seo\/ -->\n<title>Amazon to sell cars online, starting with Hyundai | TechCrunch<\/title>\n<meta name=\"description\" content=\"It was inevitable. news includes all the present and future ways people and packages get from Point A to Point B. Coverage includes scooters and e-bikes to autonomous vehicles, EVs, transit, and evTOLs. We cover auto tech players big and small, from Tesla, GM, Uber, and Lyft, to small startups entering the automotive tech space.","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/category\/transportation\/","name":"Transportation","slug":"transportation","taxonomy":"category","parent":0,"yoast_head":"<!-- This site is optimized with the Yoast SEO Premium plugin v20.2.1 (Yoast SEO v20.2.1) - https:\/\/yoast.com\/wordpress\/plugins\/seo\/ -->\n<title>Transportation &amp; Auto News | TechCrunch<\/title>\n<meta name=\"description\" content=\"Read the latest on transportation and auto news, from scooters and e-bikes to autonomous vehicles and EVs. href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?categories=2401"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?categories=2401"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?categories=2401"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?categories=2401"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2628843,"date":"2023-11-16T09:09:52","date_gmt":"2023-11-16T17:09:52","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2628843"},"modified":"2023-11-16T11:31:38","modified_gmt":"2023-11-16T19:31:38","slug":"lucid-gravity-suv-ev","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/lucid-gravity-suv-ev\/","title":{"rendered":"The all-electric Lucid Gravity SUV finally makes its debut with 440 miles of range"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">EV maker Lucid took the long-awaited wraps off the Gravity, an all-electric three-row SUV with a range that could hypothetically leave a Tesla Model X, Fisker Ocean or Rivian R1S stuck behind at a charging station.<\/p>\n<p>The importance of the Lucid Gravity \u2014 and more aptly the reception it receives \u2014 is existential for the EV maker. The Lucid Air sedan, the company&#8217;s first EV, has received positive reviews. However, demand has softened this year, forcing the automaker to slash prices and <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/lucid-revises-2023-production-amid-softening-demand-for-luxury-evs\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">lower its production guidance<\/a> for the year.<\/p>\n<p>The Gravity could help Lucid regain a foothold in the increasingly competitive EV industry. Of course, Lucid has to actually launch the vehicle. Lucid <a href=\"https:\/\/robbreport.com\/motors\/cars\/lucid-air-ev-delayed-covid-1234598912\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">delayed<\/a> its sedan, and its SUV is no different in this regard. The company originally aimed to start making Gravity SUVs in late 2023, but in August, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/08\/07\/lucid-plans-to-start-production-of-gravity-electric-suv-in-late-2024\/\">moved the production date<\/a> to late 2024.<\/p>\n<h2>Lucid Gravity basics<\/h2>\n<p><div id=\"attachment_2630043\" style=\"width: 1034px\" class=\"wp-caption alignnone\"><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg\"><img fetchpriority=\"high\" decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2630043\" class=\"size-full wp-image-2630043\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg\" alt=\"Lucid-Gravity-terrain\" width=\"1024\" height=\"576\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg 2000w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg?resize=150,84 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg?resize=300,169 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg?resize=768,432 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg?resize=680,383 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg?resize=1536,864 1536w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg?resize=1200,675 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-terrain.jpg?resize=50,28 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><\/a><p id=\"caption-attachment-2630043\" class=\"wp-caption-text\"><strong>Image Credits:<\/strong> Lucid<\/p><\/div><\/p>\n<p>Lucid wrote bombastically that the EV &#8220;heralds the dawn of a new era for electric SUVs.&#8221; There&#8217;s an explanation for this tone; as sales of Lucid&#8217;s first vehicle (a luxury sedan called Air) <a href=\"https:\/\/www.reuters.com\/technology\/lucid-expects-make-least-10000-electric-cars-2023-2023-02-22\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">fall short<\/a>, its future now rides on whether Gravity can actually be a force in the EV market. This is a new era for Lucid, however. And the all-electric SUV it designed has some noteworthy qualities, including a stout low-slung design with a sport tail out the back \u2014 all of which contributes to a coefficient drag of 0.24. That aerodynamic design is one factor that contributes to its battery range. Lucid also claims the vehicle goes from zero to 60 in less than 3.5 seconds \u2014 about <a href=\"https:\/\/www.tesla.com\/modelx\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">a second longer<\/a> than what Tesla claims for its Model X Plaid.<\/p>\n<p><div id=\"attachment_2630106\" style=\"width: 1034px\" class=\"wp-caption alignnone\"><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg\"><img decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2630106\" class=\"size-full wp-image-2630106\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg\" alt=\"lucid gravity ceo designer\" width=\"1024\" height=\"768\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg 2000w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg?resize=150,113 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg?resize=300,225 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg?resize=768,576 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg?resize=680,510 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg?resize=1536,1152 1536w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg?resize=1200,900 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/lucid-gravity-ceo-designer.jpg?resize=50,38 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><\/a><p id=\"caption-attachment-2630106\" class=\"wp-caption-text\">Lucid CEO Peter Rawlinson and head designer Derek Jenkins sit in the frunk of a the new Lucid Gravity. <strong>Image Credits:<\/strong> Harri Weber<\/p><\/div><\/p>\n<p>The Lucid Gravity features a compact powertrain with EV motors and a 900-volt electrical architecture and<span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\"> payload capacity of over 1,500 pounds and an additional 6,000 pounds\u00a0<\/span>of towing capacity, the company claims. Lucid estimates Gravity&#8217;s range will top out beyond 440 miles. Lucid boss Peter Rawlinson said in a statement that the projected range is possible &#8220;with a battery pack a little more than half the size of some of our battery-hungry competitors.&#8221; If it performs as Lucid suggests in real-world conditions, it&#8217;ll be the longest-range electric SUV on the market.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>The vehicle can also apparently charge like a Tesla \u2014 gulping down as much as 200 miles in range after about 15 minutes at a fast charger. The company says the seven-seater SUV will have a starting price under $80,000. If history is a guide, there will be plenty of add-ons and trim levels that will push the price well beyond that number.<\/p>\n<h2>Inside the Lucid Gravity<\/h2>\n<p><div id=\"attachment_2630051\" style=\"width: 1034px\" class=\"wp-caption alignnone\"><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg\"><img decoding=\"async\" aria-describedby=\"caption-attachment-2630051\" class=\"size-full wp-image-2630051\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg\" alt=\"Lucid-Gravity-SUV inside\" width=\"1024\" height=\"768\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg 2000w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg?resize=150,113 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg?resize=300,225 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg?resize=768,576 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg?resize=680,510 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg?resize=1536,1152 1536w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg?resize=1200,900 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2023\/11\/Lucid-Gravity-.jpeg?resize=50,38 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><\/a><p id=\"caption-attachment-2630051\" class=\"wp-caption-text\"><strong>Image Credits:<\/strong> Lucid<\/p><\/div><\/p>\n<p>Inside the SUV is a 34-inch curved OLED screen that floats above a redesigned steering wheel. Lucid amps up the interior touches, as it did with the Air.<\/p>\n<p>Of note is the seven-passenger configuration and a console that slides open for additional storage. Lucid said those sliding second-row seats combine with integrated convenience tables for an &#8220;elevated back-seat experience.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p>Those back seats, both second and third rows, fold flat to provide more than 112 cubic feet of total usable cargo space, according to the company.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>EV maker Lucid took the long-awaited wraps off the Gravity, an all-electric three-row SUV with a range that could hypothetically leave a Tesla Model X, Fisker Ocean or Rivian R1S stuck behind at a charging station. The importance of the Lucid Gravity \u2014 and more aptly the reception it receives \u2014 is existential for the [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574547,"featured_media":2630084,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"07f8bf76-6170-37d8-8d53-c97ef97dab7d","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-16T17:09:58Z","apple_news_api_id":"b7a43797-899d-4df4-aaa9-d48cd1c41fe3","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-16T19:31:33Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAw==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/At6Q3l4mdTfSqqdSM0cQf4w","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[197443,76801,316925],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- runch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=316925"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=316925"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=316925"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=316925"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2629738,"date":"2023-11-16T04:00:56","date_gmt":"2023-11-16T12:00:56","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2629738"},"modified":"2023-11-16T16:37:51","modified_gmt":"2023-11-17T00:37:51","slug":"toyota-turning-to-redwood-materials-critical-battery-materials-us-factory","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/16\/toyota-turning-to-redwood-materials-critical-battery-materials-us-factory\/","title":{"rendered":"Toyota is turning to startup Redwood Materials for critical battery materials"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Battery cells produced at Toyota&#8217;s future North American EV factory will someday contain a little Redwood Materials DNA.<\/p>\n<p>The two companies announced Thursday that Redwood Materials will supply Toyota with cathode material and anode copper foil for battery cells produced at the automaker&#8217;s <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/31\/toyota-to-invest-another-8b-into-north-carolina-ev-battery-factory\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">$13.9 billion factory<\/a> in North Carolina that&#8217;s slated to go into production in 2025. The deal is valuable to Redwood. But it&#8217;s also a win for Toyota, which now has a U.S. source for two critical components that account for the majority of the cost of a battery cell and are manufactured entirely overseas.<\/p>\n<p>While Redwood and Toyota didn&#8217;t disclose the terms of the agreement, it likely parallels a <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/11\/15\/redwood-materials-to-supply-panasonic-with-cathode-material-in-multi-billion-dollar-deal\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">similar deal with Panasonic<\/a> that is worth several billion dollars.<\/p>\n<p>The deal marks an <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/06\/21\/redwood-materials-partners-with-toyota-to-recycle-batteries-in-us\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">expansion of a partnership<\/a> between Redwood Materials and Toyota that was announced in June 2022. Under that initial partnership, Redwood Materials agreed to refurbish or recycle batteries from Toyota\u2019s hybrid and electrified vehicles. For batteries that can&#8217;t be refurbished, Redwood pulls out materials such as copper, lithium, cobalt and nickel and then remanufactures those materials into components that can be returned to Toyota for cell manufacturing.<\/p>\n<p>The effort was announced as the first batch of Toyota Prius vehicles that were released 20 years ago retired from the road. And it will only grow. Toyota&#8217;s battery lifecycle ecosystem is forecast to include the recycling, remanufacturing and repurposing of the nearly five million current operating units, according to the automaker.<\/p>\n<p>This latest deal ups the ante \u2014 and the value \u2014 for <a href=\"https:\/\/redwoodmaterials.com\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Redwood Materials<\/a>, a lithium-ion battery recycling and materials startup founded by former Tesla CTO JB Straubel. Under a long-term agreement, Redwood will now supply cathode active material and copper foil produced at its U.S. facilities. The cathode materials supplied to Toyota at its North Carolina factory will include a minimum of 20% recycled nickel, 20% recycled lithium and 50% recycled cobalt, as well as 100% recycled copper in the anode copper foil.<\/p>\n<p>Lithium-ion batteries contain three critical building blocks. There are two electrodes, an anode (negative) on one side and a cathode (positive) on the other. Typically, an electrolyte sits in the middle and acts as the courier to move ions between the electrodes when charging and discharging. Cathode foils, which account for more than half the cost of a battery cell, contain lithium, nickel and cobalt. Redwood is able to capture all of those materials through its battery recycling and processing.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>Redwood continues to expand its Carson City, Nevada headquarters and is expected to break ground on a second battery materials campus in South Carolina later this year. Both campuses will recycle, refine and manufacture battery materials, aiming to scale production of components at a projected volume of 100 gigawatt-hour per year\u2019s worth of materials, enough for one million electric vehicles, by 2025.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Battery cells produced at Toyota&#8217;s future North American EV factory will someday contain a little Redwood Materials DNA. The two companies announced Thursday that Redwood Materials will supply Toyota with cathode material and anode copper foil for battery cells produced at the automaker&#8217;s $13.9 billion factory in North Carolina that&#8217;s slated to go into production [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2629840,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"5eabc5ed-b77e-35f2-b5b4-f6199abe5a16","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-16T12:01:23Z","apple_news_api_id":"3cc12ecc-ea63-4822-a484-d3a05ea4bd42","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-17T00:37:45Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/APMEuzOpjSCKkhNOgXqS9Qg","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[576957003,2401],"tags":[576919989,197443,76801,449561028,13804],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- nch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=13804"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=13804"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=13804"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=13804"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2629191,"date":"2023-11-15T07:11:55","date_gmt":"2023-11-15T15:11:55","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2629191"},"modified":"2023-11-15T09:53:55","modified_gmt":"2023-11-15T17:53:55","slug":"arrival-secures-emergency-cash-so-it-can-sell-itself","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/15\/arrival-secures-emergency-cash-so-it-can-sell-itself\/","title":{"rendered":"Arrival secures emergency cash so it can sell itself"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\">Arrival, the once buzzy EV startup that went public via a merger with a blank check company, is in its final death throes.<\/p>\n<p>The company has secured a $50 million bridge loan, funds that will keep it afloat long enough to explore a potential sale, according to a <a href=\"https:\/\/arrival.gcs-web.com\/node\/8821\/html#i4a4a24e86a4a43f9bae2cd00272a7834_162\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">regulatory filing<\/a> posted Wednesday. The additional liquidity, which has a term of 120 days, is being provided by Antara Capital and Highbridge Capital Management.<\/p>\n<p>Arrival plans to the use the bridge financing along with its remaining cash on hand to find a buyer or other &#8220;strategic alternative transaction.&#8221; In other words, Arrival has been given a tight framework to sell the company and pay back companies and people it&#8217;s indebted to. It&#8217;s the equivalent of lending someone $5 to buy enough gas to drive the car down to the dealer and sell it.<\/p>\n<div>\n<p>Arrival was once a high-flying EV startup with <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/03\/this-ev-startup-was-going-to-revolutionize-how-cars-were-made-now-its-on-life-support\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">an ambitious plan<\/a> to use microfactories to build electric buses, vans and even a car designed for Uber drivers. Those plans are now just distant images in the rearview mirror.<\/p>\n<p>In the past 15 months, the company has laid off workers four times, slashed production targets and dropped its Uber car and bus programs. It has also failed to meet Securities and Exchange Commission filing requirements, most recently <a href=\"https:\/\/www.sec.gov\/Archives\/edgar\/data\/1835059\/000162828023036709\/a6-kstaffdeterminationnoti.htm#i8f5d289fbf774e54abb4faea3f62384d_13\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">missing another deadline<\/a> to file its 2022 annual report.<\/p>\n<\/div>\n<p>Arrival executives have also looked for more capital to keep the company alive. Arrival <span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\">secured a <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/03\/13\/arrival-nabs-300m-lifeline-but-is-still-seeking-more-funds\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">$300 million lifeline<\/a> back in March 2023 to help it stay in business through the end of 2023 as it sought <\/span><span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\">additional dedicated funds to develop its XL delivery vans for the U.S. market and start production in Charlotte, North Carolina by 2024. The company also took other measures such as a reverse stock split<\/span>\u00a0to help it\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/11\/03\/ev-maker-arrival-gets-delisting-warning-from-nasdaq\/\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2022\/11\/03\/ev-maker-arrival-gets-delisting-warning-from-nasdaq\/\">regain compliance<\/a>\u00a0with the Nasdaq.<\/p>\n<p>None of those schemes has worked.<\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p>Arrival said in its Wednesday filing it ended the first half of 2023 with about <span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\">$43 million of cash and cash equivalents. The company reported a net loss of $155.7 million in the first half of the year, compared to a loss $100 million in the same period in 2022. Arrival has tried to reduce costs through restructuring efforts that involved <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/10\/10\/cash-strapped-ev-maker-arrival-lays-off-yet-more-workers\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">laying off<\/a> the bulk of its employees. It also reduced capital expenditures to $4.1 million in the first half of 2023, compared to <\/span><span style=\"font-size: 1rem; letter-spacing: -0.1px;\">$198.9 million in the first half of 2022.<\/span><\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Arrival, the once buzzy EV startup that went public via a merger with a blank check company, is in its final death throes. The company has secured a $50 million bridge loan, funds that will keep it afloat long enough to explore a potential sale, according to a regulatory filing posted Wednesday. The additional liquidity, [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2612763,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"54893c82-ef37-3417-a8b2-9d50cbb3a4bd","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-15T15:12:00Z","apple_news_api_id":"b9e1af9b-d36a-43ee-8d4e-c71078772bcb","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-15T17:51:15Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AueGvm9NqQ-6NTscQeHcryw","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[576827789,197443,76801],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- nch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_battlefield?tags=76801"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc-media-gallery?tags=76801"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_unified_video?tags=76801"},{"href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-json\/wp\/v2\/tc_video?tags=76801"}],"curies":[{"name":"wp","href":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/api.w.org\/{rel}","templated":true}]}}],[],[],[],[],[]]}},{"id":2627805,"date":"2023-11-12T17:59:06","date_gmt":"2023-11-13T01:59:06","guid":{"rendered":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/?p=2627805"},"modified":"2023-11-13T09:02:50","modified_gmt":"2023-11-13T17:02:50","slug":"cruise-spirals-and-ltas-airship-breaks-cover-in-silicon-valley","status":"publish","type":"post","link":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/12\/cruise-spirals-and-ltas-airship-breaks-cover-in-silicon-valley\/","title":{"rendered":"Cruise spirals and LTA&#8217;s airship breaks cover in Silicon Valley"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\"><em>The Station is a weekly newsletter dedicated to all things transportation.\u00a0Sign up here \u2014\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/newsletters\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/newsletters\">just click The Station<\/a>\u00a0\u2014 to receive the newsletter every weekend in your inbox. Subscribe for free.<\/em><\/p>\n<p>Welcome back to The Station, your central hub for all past, present and future means of moving people and packages from Point A to Point B.<\/p>\n<p>This week, we&#8217;ll keep things short due to the Veteran&#8217;s Day holiday; don&#8217;t worry, we still have plenty for you to catch up on.<\/p>\n<p>But first, one fun thing.<\/p>\n<p><strong>LTA Research<\/strong>, the Sergey Brin-backed startup, took the wraps off its prototype electric airship \u2014 the largest aircraft in the world \u2014 right in the heart of Silicon Valley. As contributor <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/08\/the-worlds-largest-aircraft-breaks-cover-in-silicon-valley\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Mark Harris wrote<\/a>, the airship &#8212; its snow-white steampunk profile visible from the busy 101 highway &#8212; has taken drone technology such as fly-by-wire controls, electric motors and lidar sensing, and supersized them to something longer than three Boeing 737s, potentially able to carry tons of cargo over many hundreds of miles.<\/p>\n<p><em>Let&#8217;s go!<\/em><\/p>\n<hr \/>\n<p><em>Want to reach out with a tip, comment or complaint? Email Kirsten at <a href=\"mailto:kirsten.korosec@techcrunch.com\">kirsten.korosec@techcrunch.com<\/a>.<\/em><\/p><div class=\"piano-inline-promo\"><\/div>\n<p><em>Reminder that you can drop us a\u00a0note at\u00a0<a href=\"mailto:tips@techcrunch.com\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">tips@techcrunch.com<\/a>.\u00a0<strong>If you prefer to remain anonymous<\/strong>,\u00a0<\/em><a href=\"https:\/\/link.techcrunch.com\/click\/30325329.4639\/aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS9nb3QtYS10aXAvP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09VENuZXdzbGV0dGVyJnRwY2M9VEN0cmFuc3BvcnRhdGlvbm5ld3NsZXR0ZXI\/606b5c505a5ea2367772122aBd61e79c3\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><em>click here to contact us<\/em><\/a><em>, which includes SecureDrop (<\/em><a href=\"https:\/\/link.techcrunch.com\/click\/30325329.4639\/aHR0cHM6Ly90ZWNoY3J1bmNoLmNvbS9zZWN1cmVkcm9wP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09VENuZXdzbGV0dGVyJnRwY2M9VEN0cmFuc3BvcnRhdGlvbm5ld3NsZXR0ZXI\/606b5c505a5ea2367772122aB80a9346e\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><em>instructions here<\/em><\/a><em>) and various encrypted messaging apps.<\/em><\/p>\n<h2>Micromobbin&#8217;<\/h2>\n<p><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png\"><img fetchpriority=\"high\" decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone wp-image-1894192 size-full\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png\" alt=\"the station scooter1a\" width=\"1600\" height=\"401\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png 1600w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=150,38 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=300,75 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=768,192 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=680,170 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=1536,385 1536w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=1200,301 1200w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/the-station-scooter1a.png?resize=50,13 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1600px) 100vw, 1600px\" \/><\/a><\/p>\n<p><strong>Ridepanda<\/strong>, the D2C micromobility marketplace that a couple of Bird, Lime and Scoot alumni launched in 2020, has made a <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/ridepandas-shift-from-d2c-micromobility-platform-to-corporate-e-bike-supplier\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">significant business pivot<\/a> that focuses on enterprise-level business. Now, instead of selling a range of e-bike and e-scooter models from top brands to customers across the country, Ridepanda has become an end-to-end solution for employers &#8212; like Amazon and Google &#8212; to offer employees commuter benefits.<\/p>\n<p>The key takeaways here are 1) What wasn&#8217;t working with D2C; and 2) Why they chose B2B2E. The tl;dr is that getting the supply chain and servicing set up for multiple different brands all over the U.S. was incredibly difficult, and enterprises are doing the most right now to get people back into the office and meet sustainability goals.<\/p>\n<hr \/>\n<p>Tech continues to seep into micromobility. For instance,\u00a0<strong>Gogoro<\/strong>, the Taiwanese two-wheeler\u00a0battery-swapping giant, said that its smart electric scooters can now be unlocked and turned on <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/gogoros-smartscooters-can-now-be-unlocked-via-apple-wallet\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">via a key<\/a> in a rider\u2019s Apple Wallet on their iPhone or Apple Watch. Vehicle owners will also be able to share their scooter key with other iOS users.<\/p>\n<p style=\"text-align: right;\"><em>\u2014 Rebecca Bellan<\/em><\/p>\n<h2>Deal of the week<\/h2>\n<p><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png\"><img decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone wp-image-1896226 size-full\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png\" alt=\"money the station\" width=\"1024\" height=\"256\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png 1024w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=150,38 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=300,75 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=768,192 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=680,170 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/10\/money-the-station.png?resize=50,13 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><\/a><\/p>\n<p>Robotaxi companies Cruise and Waymo get the bulk of the attention \u2014 and lately, not the good kind. Meanwhile, another autonomous vehicle startup called <strong>May Mobility<\/strong> has been <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/may-mobility-rides-the-av-wave-while-cruise-and-waymo-feel-the-heat\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">operating quietly in the background<\/a>, which co-founder and CEO Edwin Olson told TechCrunch is by design.<\/p>\n<p>That under-the-radar approach \u2014 which is likely helped by its B2B business model \u2014 hasn&#8217;t prevented it from raising money. May Mobility raised $105 million in a Series D round led by Japan\u2019s telecom giant NTT.\u00a0 Toyota Ventures, BMW i Ventures, Trucks VC and State Farm Ventures also participated in the deal, among other investors. To date, the company has raised around $300 million.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Other deals that got my attention &#8230;<\/strong><\/p>\n<p><strong>EasyPark Group<\/strong>, a mobile paid parking company, said it intends to acquire Flowbird Group, which operates under brands Flowbird, YourParkingSpace, TPARK, Extenso Cloud and Yellowbrick. Flowbird offers a number of services such as pay and display machines, software and &#8220;park and charge.&#8221;<\/p>\n<p><strong>Flitter<\/strong>, a Paris-based pay-by-mile insurance tech startup, <a href=\"https:\/\/tech.eu\/2023\/11\/06\/insurtech-startup-flitter-raises-eur35m-for-expansion-and-profitability-by-2024\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">raised \u20ac3.5 million<\/a> ($3.75 million) in seed funding led by Swiss insurer Helvetia. The Frenchfounders fund, former Sarenza CEO St\u00e9phane Treppoz, Optimind founder Christophe Eberl\u00e9, Voodoo co-founder Laurent Ritter and Sorare co-founder Adrien Montfort also participated.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Getir,<\/strong> the Turkish instant grocery delivery startup, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/getir-freshdirect\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">acquired<\/a> FreshDirect, an online grocery delivery service based in New York.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Niron Magnetics<\/strong>, a Minnesota-based startup developing rare earth-free permanent magnets, raised $33 million with new investments from GM Ventures and Stellantis Ventures. Previous investors Shakopee Mdewakanton Sioux Community and the University of Minnesota also participated. The company plans to use the funds to expand its pilot production facilities and scale manufacturing capacity of its &#8220;clean earth magnet.&#8221; The CEO told me the company plans to double its 60-person workforce by the end of 2023 and double it again in 2024.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Zeekr<\/strong>, another Geely company, is gearing up for a <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/zeekr-us-ipo-china-risks\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">U.S. IPO<\/a>.<\/p>\n<h2>Notable reads and other tidbits<\/h2>\n<h3><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png\"><img decoding=\"async\" class=\"alignnone wp-image-1926823 size-full\" src=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png\" alt=\"\" width=\"1024\" height=\"257\" srcset=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png 1024w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=150,38 150w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=300,75 300w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=768,193 768w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=680,171 680w, https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/wp-content\/uploads\/2019\/12\/the-station-ride-hailing1.png?resize=50,13 50w\" sizes=\"(max-width: 1024px) 100vw, 1024px\" \/><\/a><\/h3>\n<h3>Autonomous vehicles<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Cruise<\/strong>, and its parent company <strong>GM<\/strong>, continue to deal with the aftermath of an October 2 incident that left a woman, who was initially hit by a human driver, stuck under and then dragged by a robotaxi. This week alone, the company <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/06\/gm-halts-production-of-cruise-origin-robotaxi-amid-suspended-operations\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">paused production<\/a> of its custom-built Origin vehicle, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/08\/cruise-recalls-entire-fleet-after-robotaxi-ran-over-dragged-pedestrian\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">recalled its fleet<\/a> of 950 vehicle equipped with autonomous vehicle software and <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/cruise-begins-layoffs-starting-with-workers-who-supported-driverless-operations\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">laid off contract workers<\/a> who were responsible for the maintenance and driverless operations. Meanwhile, the <strong>California Public Utilities Commission<\/strong> issued an order to <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/cpuc-consider-redoing-hearing-to-grant-cruises-24-7-robotaxi-permit\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">halt the implementation of Cruise&#8217;s permit<\/a> to charge for robotaxi rides across San Francisco 24\/7 as they consider the city&#8217;s request for a redo of the hearing that granted the permit.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Ghost Autonomy<\/strong> said it plans to begin exploring the applications of multimodal large language models (LLMs) &#8212; AI models that can understand text as well as images &#8212; in self-driving through the OpenAI Startup Fund, which provides early access to OpenAI systems and Azure resources from Microsoft, OpenAI&#8217;s close collaborator, plus a $5 million investment. TechCrunch reporter Kyle Wiggers found <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/ghost-now-openai-backed-claims-llms-will-overcome-self-driving-setbacks-but-experts-are-skeptical\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">experts were skeptical<\/a> and said LLMs are the wrong tool for self driving.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Motional<\/strong> and <strong>Uber Eats<\/strong> are teaming up to autonomously deliver food from Shake Shack.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Waymo<\/strong> started winter testing its robotaxis in Buffalo, New York.<\/p>\n<h3>Electric vehicles, batteries &amp; charging<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Ram<\/strong> introduced the <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/06\/the-2025-ram-ramcharger-is-an-electric-truck-that-gets-its-ev-juice-from-a-gas-powered-generator\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">2025 Ramcharger<\/a> \u2014 an electric truck that gets its EV juice from a gas-powered generator. Based on feedback from some readers, this will be a polarizing vehicle. Some people were incredibly excited and called it the perfect truck; others took offense to the EV branding.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Polestar<\/strong> held its inaugural Polestar Day to showcase its<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/10\/polestar-tackles-softening-ev-demand-with-new-tech-and-next-gen-vehicles\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"> vision for the future<\/a>: new tech and next-generation vehicles that the Swedish EV company owned by China&#8217;s Geely Holdings hopes will spark sales and spur an era of growth. But as contributor Abigail Bassett noted, the event stood in stark contrast to its present.<\/p>\n<h3>Earnings<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Lucid<\/strong> didn&#8217;t have the rosiest of earnings reports. The company revised its 2023 production outlook amid softening demand for luxury electric vehicles. Meanwhile, the company jumped on the <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/06\/lucid-adopts-teslas-charging-standard\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">Tesla charging standard<\/a> bandwagon.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Lyft<\/strong> reported 22.4 million active riders in the third quarter, up from 21.5 million in the second quarter and 19.6 million in the first. The growth suggests that its\u00a0strategy of slashing ride-hail fares to compete with Uber has resulted in slow and steady gains for the company, but competition remains fierce. Get the <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/08\/lyfts-price-war-with-uber-yields-mixed-results\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">full Q3 rundown<\/a> here.\u00a0The ride-hailing company also said it will introduce a \u201cmore affordable sort of higher-end ride\u201d in an attempt to offer ride-hail products that <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/08\/lyft-picks-comfort-over-sharing-in-bid-to-compete-with-uber\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">customers actually want<\/a>, according to the CEO.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Rivian<\/strong> continued to close the gap on losses, reduce costs and ramp up production in the <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/rivian-raises-production-forecast-for-2023-narrows-losses-in-q3\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">third quarter<\/a> with results that beat Wall Street expectations and suggested a rosier future, including raising its annual production guidance from 52,000 to 54,000 vehicles. The big news: Rivian and Amazon are<a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/rivian-and-amazon-are-no-longer-exclusive\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"> no longer exclusive<\/a>. Oh, and Rivian founder and CEO <strong>RJ Scaringe<\/strong> also <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/08\/rivian-eyes-software-upcharges-around-ar-and-autonomy-not-heated-seats\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">weighed in on subscriptions<\/a>. Tl;dr: he&#8217;s eyeing software upcharges around AR and autonomy, not heated seats.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Uber&#8217;s <\/strong><a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/ubers-q3-numbers-profitability-gains\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">third-quarter earnings<\/a> show a profitable ride-hail and delivery company that&#8217;s chugging along in spite of slowing growth in some sectors.<\/p>\n<h3>Future of flight<\/h3>\n<p><strong>Archer Aviation<\/strong> partnered with India&#8217;s travel and hospitality conglomerate InterGlobe Enterprises to launch an <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/09\/archer-aviation-electric-air-taxi-india-launch-2026\/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">all-electric air taxi service<\/a> in the country in 2026.<\/p>\n<p><strong>Skyryse<\/strong>, the aviation software startup, completed a fully autorotation emergency landing procedure in a helicopter. The milestone puts the company on the path to unveil the first production helicopter featuring it technology in the first quarter\u00a0of next year.<\/p>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>The Station is a weekly newsletter dedicated to all things transportation.\u00a0Sign up here \u2014\u00a0just click The Station\u00a0\u2014 to receive the newsletter every weekend in your inbox. Subscribe for free. Welcome back to The Station, your central hub for all past, present and future means of moving people and packages from Point A to Point B. [&hellip;]<\/p>\n","protected":false},"author":133574188,"featured_media":2626524,"comment_status":"open","ping_status":"closed","sticky":false,"template":"","format":"standard","meta":{"amp_status":"","relegenceEntities":[],"relegenceSubjects":[],"carmot_uuid":"3e87e92f-ea59-360d-a5bb-34247f105edd","footnotes":"","apple_news_api_created_at":"2023-11-13T01:59:13Z","apple_news_api_id":"a807a704-a9e8-4257-a853-d2de3f8245a1","apple_news_api_modified_at":"2023-11-13T17:02:38Z","apple_news_api_revision":"AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA==","apple_news_api_share_url":"http:\/\/web.archive.org\/web\/20231201234640\/https:\/\/apple.news\/AqAenBKnoQleoU9LeP4JFoQ","apple_news_coverimage":0,"apple_news_coverimage_caption":"","apple_news_is_hidden":false,"apple_news_is_paid":false,"apple_news_is_preview":false,"apple_news_is_sponsored":false,"apple_news_maturity_rating":"","apple_news_metadata":"\"\"","apple_news_pullquote":"","apple_news_pullquote_position":"","apple_news_slug":"","apple_news_sections":"\"\"","apple_news_suppress_video_url":false,"apple_news_use_image_component":false},"categories":[2401],"tags":[576717918],"crunchbase_tag":[],"tc_stories_tax":[],"tc_ec_category":[],"tc_event":[],"tc_regions_tax":[],"yoast_head":"<!-- This site is optimized with the Yoast SEO Premium plugin v20.2.1 (Yoast SEO v20.2.1) - https:\/\/yoast.com\/wordpress\/plugins\/seo\/ -->\n<title>Cruise spirals and LTA&#039;s airship breaks cover in Silicon Valley | TechCrunch<\/title>\n<meta name=\"description\" content=\"The Station is a weekly newsletter focused on transportation. DevDay, reinventing the REIT and good actors in crypto"},"content":{"rendered":"<p id=\"speakable-summary\"><iframe class=\"embed-iframe\" src=\"https:\/\/player.simplecast.com\/2ce7da67-7837-4022-bf49-af0d445cffac?dark=true\" width=\"100%\" height=\"200px\" frameborder=\"no\" scrolling=\"no\" seamless=\"\"><\/iframe><\/p>\n<p><em>Listen here or\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/link.chtbl.com\/Equity-q4-podposts?sid=Techcrunch\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">wherever you get your podcasts<\/a>.<\/em><\/p>\n<p>Hello and welcome back to\u00a0<a href=\"https:\/\/podcasts.apple.com\/us\/podcast\/equity\/id1215439780\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\" data-mrf-link=\"https:\/\/podcasts.apple.com\/us\/podcast\/equity\/id1215439780\">Equity<\/a>, a podcast about the business of startups, where we unpack the numbers and nuance behind the headlines.<\/p>\n<p>This is our Friday show, and we\u2019re talking about the week\u2019s biggest startup and tech news. This time &#8217;round we had Kirsten Korosec, Mary Ann Azevedo and Alex Wilhelm on the job to chat through a massive pile of news:<\/p>\n<ul>\n<li>For everyone who listened to our fintech deep-dive, <a href=\"https:\/\/investors.affirm.com\/news-events\/events-and-presentations\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\">here are Affirm&#8217;s results<\/a>.<\/li>\n<li>Deals of the Week: <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/may-mobility-rides-the-av-wave-while-cruise-and-waymo-feel-the-heat\/\">$105 million for May Mobility<\/a>, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/08\/mogul-club-raises-3-6m-toward-its-effort-to-make-real-estate-investing-more-accessible\/\">$3.6 million for Mogul Club<\/a> and <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/microsoft-partners-with-vcs-to-give-startups-free-ai-chip-access\/\">Microsoft&#8217;s latest startup wooing trend<\/a>.<\/li>\n<li>WeWork is <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/06\/wework-once-worth-47-billion-files-for-bankruptcy\/\">bankrupt<\/a>, and we are <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/07\/wework-bankruptcy-business-bad\/\">Not Shocked<\/a>.<\/li>\n<li>All things <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/06\/everything-announced-at-openais-first-developer-event\/\">from OpenAI&#8217;s developer day<\/a>, and how its latest news is a <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/06\/get-the-pdf-outta-here\/\">good example of platform risk<\/a>.<\/li>\n<li>It&#8217;s <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/08\/klarna-ipo-incoming\/\">raining IPOs<\/a>! <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/06\/shein-reportedly-seeks-90-billion-valuation-in-ipo\/\">Here<\/a>, <a href=\"https:\/\/techcrunch.com\/2023\/11\/08\/circle-ipo-makes-sense\/?tpcc=tcplustwitter\">there<\/a>, everywhere!<\/li>\n<\/ul>\n<p>And with that, we&#8217;re going to go rest for the weekend and come back Monday at full steam!<\/p>\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<p><i>For episode transcripts and more, head to\u00a0<\/i><a href=\"https:\/\/equity.simplecast.com\/episodes\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><i>Equity\u2019s Simplecast website<\/i><\/a><i>.<\/i><\/p>\n<div class=\"article-content\">\n<p><i>Equity drops at 7 a.m. PT every Monday, Wednesday and Friday, so subscribe to us on\u00a0<\/i><a href=\"https:\/\/itunes.apple.com\/us\/podcast\/id1215439780\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><i>Apple Podcasts<\/i><\/a><i>,<\/i><a href=\"https:\/\/overcast.fm\/itunes1215439780\/equity\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><i>\u00a0Overcast<\/i><\/a><i>,<\/i><a href=\"https:\/\/open.spotify.com\/show\/5IEYLip3eDppcOmy5DmphC?si=rZDFHv2sQUul_g94iCRgpQ\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><i>\u00a0Spotify<\/i><\/a><i>\u00a0and\u00a0all the casts. TechCrunch also has a\u00a0<\/i><a href=\"https:\/\/podcasts.apple.com\/us\/podcast\/chain-reaction\/id1616008890\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><i>great show on crypto<\/i><\/a><i>, a\u00a0<\/i><a href=\"https:\/\/podcasts.apple.com\/us\/podcast\/found\/id1561051074\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener\"><i>show that interviews founders<\/i><\/a><i>\u00a0<\/i><i>and more!<\/i><\/p>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n<\/div>\n","protected":false},"excerpt":{"rendered":"<p>Welcome back to\u00a0Equity, a podcast about the business of startups, where we unpack the numbers and nuance behind the headlines.
